zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Lovell於大約1280年出生在英國瓦維其可爾維斯頓-易-亞登時，她的父親John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel26歲，母親Isabel Bois25歲。她於1296年2月15日之前與William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1346年在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Lovell于大约1280年出生在英国瓦維其可爾維斯頓-易-亞登时，她的父亲John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel26岁，母亲Isabel Bois25岁。她于1296年2月15日之前与William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1346年在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Lovell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lovel ‘wolf cub’, a diminutive of lou, love ‘wolf’. Compare Love and Low .

variants.name: Lowell
meanings: English: variant of Lovell , derived from Anglo-Norman Frenchlou ‘wolf’ + the diminutive suffix -el.

History: Lowell is the surname of one of America's most distinguished NewEngland families, which have been prominent for over 200 years. Itsfounder, John Lowell (1743–1802), was a legislator and judge. Thecity of Lowell, MA was named in honor of his son Francis Cabot Lowell(1775–1817), a textile manufacturer.

variants.name: Love
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name derived from the Old English female name Lufu ‘love’, or the masculine equivalent Lufa. Compare Leaf 2.

English and Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French love ‘she-wolf’ or simply ‘wolf’, the word love being a back-formation from the diminutive forms lovel and lovet ‘little wolf’. See also Low 3, from Anglo-Norman French lou, the more usual form of the word.
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionghuin (see McKinnon ).

variants.name: Loveall
meanings: English: presumably a variant of   Lovell  , or possibly a habitational name from Lovehall in Tayside.

variants.name: Lovewell
meanings: English (mainly Norfolk): from Middle English love ‘love’ (Old English lufian ‘to love’) + wel ‘well’ (Old English wel), presumably denoting a lover or philanderer. There may have been some confusion with Lovell .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Lovell was born about 1280, in Weston-in-Arden, Warwickshire, England, her father, John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel, was 26 and her mother, Isabel Bois, was 25. She married William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche before 15 February 1296, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1346, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Lovell est née environ 1280, à Weston-in-Arden, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Isabel Bois, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche avant 15 février 1296, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1346, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Lovell um 1280 in Weston-in-Arden, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel 26 und ihre Mutter Isabel Bois 25. Sie hat vor 15. Februar 1296 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1346 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England und wurde in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Lovell è nata il circa 1280 nel Weston-in-Arden, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Isabel Bois, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche prima del 15 febbraio 1296, nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1346, nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Lovellが約1280年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウエストン-イン-アルデンで生まれた時，父John Lovell 1st Baron Lovelは26歳，母Isabel Boisは25歳であった。1296年2月15日以前にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスでWilliam la Zouche 1st Baron Zoucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1346年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 애스h비 데 라 즈우치
ko.lifeSummary: Maud Lovell(이)가 대략 1280년 영국 워윅실르 웨스토-이-아르든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Bois(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1296년 2월 15일 이전 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1346년 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Lovell nasceu aproximadamente 1280, em Weston-in-Arden, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Bois, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche antes 15 de fevereiro de 1296, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1346, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Engla: ашби де ла зоуч, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Lovell родилась около 1280 г. в местности вестон-ин-арден, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Bois, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche перед 15 февраль 1296 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1346 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Lovell nació aproximadamente 1280, en Weston-in-Arden, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Lovell 1st Baron Lovel, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Isabel Bois, tenía 25 años. Se casó con William la Zouche 1st Baron Zouche antes de 15 de febrero de 1296, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1346, en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
